articles.title: The Impact of Tariffs on the AI Data Center Buildout: Balancing Supply Chain Security and AI Infrastructure Leadership
articles.source: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)
articles.snippet: Blanket semiconductor and metal tariffs are crippling the buildout of American data center infrastructure. A more tailored regime can instead reinforce national security and ensure U.S. leadership in the AI race.

articles.title: The Iran Conflict Illuminates Taiwan’s Unique Energy Security Challenge
articles.source: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)
articles.snippet: The Iran war has exposed key challenges in Taiwan’s energy system. Taipei needs to address these challenges to successfully balance the need for more electricity, emissions mitigation, and greater resilience against supply disruptions.

articles.title: Overturning the Endangerment Finding Threatens Americans’ Health, Wealth, and Security
articles.source: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)
articles.snippet: In April, the rescission of the endangerment finding went into effect, eliminating the basis for the United States’ regulation of greenhouse gas emissions. Policymakers should not accept the long-term damage to Americans’ health in exchange for short-term economic gain.

articles.title: How Are Perceptions of the U.S.-Mexico Relationship Changing?
articles.source: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)
articles.snippet: CSIS Americas Program Senior Associate Andrew Rudman analyzes evolving Mexican perceptions of the United States and their impact on bilateral relations.

articles.title: How Might the Trump-Xi Summit Impact U.S. Farmers?
articles.source: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)
articles.snippet: The U.S. agricultural industry has borne the brunt of the recent U.S.-China trade conflict. Now, farmers watch closely as Presidents Trump and Xi seek a potential agreement in Beijing, but longer-term structural trends make a quick fix unlikely.

articles.title: A U.S. Campaign to Exploit Beijing’s Weaknesses
articles.source: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)
articles.snippet: The Chinese Communist Party has systemic weaknesses. U.S. operations should capitalize on them.

articles.title: Iran’s Nuclear Dust Clouds US Radioactive Diplomacy
articles.source: International Institute for Iranian Studies (Rasanah)
articles.snippet: The Middle East endures a fragile truce.  Over $25 billion and 13 dead soldiers later, the United States is far from clear about the obliteration of Iran’s nuclear capability and the erosion of its destabilizing power. The dual blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has emerged as Iran’s nuclear option before it acquires and unveils […]

articles.title: The Iran Conflict and the Strategic Implications for Russia
articles.source: International Institute for Iranian Studies (Rasanah)
articles.snippet: The conflict between Iran, Israel and the United States has profoundly altered the geopolitical landscape, creating a set of strategic advantages for the Russian Federation. While Moscow maintains an official stance of diplomatic caution, the material benefits derived from the Middle Eastern theater are substantial. The primary drivers of this advantage are the surge in […]

articles.title: China’s Influence Over Iran and the Constraints on Its Leverage
articles.source: International Institute for Iranian Studies (Rasanah)
articles.snippet: The Iran war has exposed the role of extra-regional powers in the Middle East, while highlighting how their responses are redefining contemporary great power politics. Regional instability has significant consequences for critical sea lanes, energy supplies and global trade, which have direct implications for energy-importing countries in Asia. In this context, China’s considerations and role […]

articles.title: Europe Confronts Strategic Exposure in the Hormuz Crisis
articles.source: International Institute for Iranian Studies (Rasanah)
articles.snippet: The prolonged crisis in the Strait of Hormuz has emerged as one of the most disruptive geopolitical developments of the 2020s. It stems from escalating tensions between Iran and a US-led coalition within the wider context of military confrontation involving Israel. For Europe, already contending with the aftershocks of the Russia-Ukraine war and the structural […]

articles.title: The Islamabad Opening: How Pakistan Became Washington and Tehran’s Key Mediator
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: Pakistan has emerged as a key mediator in US–Iran talks in Islamabad, balancing ties with both sides and regional partners. Despite limited leverage, it uses diplomacy to gain relevance, though its role remains that of facilitator amid ongoing regional tensions and economic vulnerabilities.

articles.title: The Strategic Multi-Layered Predicament of the Iran War and the Requirements for a Way Out
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: In 2026, a US-Israeli campaign against Iran escalated into a multidimensional impasse. Despite heavy strikes, Iran survives, retains leverage over the Strait of Hormuz, and carries out asymmetric responses. Ceasefire, blockade, and talks in Islamabad have failed to yield a victory narrative for either side.

articles.title: Stalemate: A Fragile Balance in the Iran War
articles.source: Al Jazeera Centre for Studies (AJCS)
articles.snippet: US efforts to impose demands on Iran by force have failed, while Iran’s attempt to enforce its own terms through selective closure of the Strait of Hormuz has also fallen short. The result is a tense stalemate that could still shift toward renewed escalation or a negotiated settlement.

articles.title: When Your Data Is Under Siege: Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence in Times of Crisis
articles.source: Bahrain Center for Strategic, International and Energy Studies (Derasat)
articles.snippet:
The battlefield is no longer confined to geographical borders; it has extended deep into digital networks and our personal devices. Amid regional tensions, citizens find themselves on the front lines—not with a weapon in hand, but with a phone in their pocket and an online bank account. This shift reflects a qualitative change in the […]
